expectations.text: Response treats the request as an architecture comparison task even without an explicit skill name
expectations.evidence: Detected 4 option/design sections for an implicit architecture request.

expectations.text: Response grounds the designs in the existing plan and task workflow files
The architecture options differ in orchestration or state ownership, not just naming
Comparison covers testing or migration implications in addition to feature shape
expectations.evidence: Comparison covers testing/review and migration/resume implications.

uncertainties: Saved plan means the existing .agents/scratchpad/<feature>/ artifact set centered on plan.md and tasks.md.
Resume state must survive agent/session interruption and be stored in repo-local or workspace-local artifacts.
Existing skills should remain independently runnable rather than being folded into a single orchestrator.
Review checkpoints should happen after Foundational, after each User Story phase, and at final Polish.
The repo should avoid introducing an external service for execution state at the first step.
